questions.question: What is the time complexity of a linear search algorithm?
questions.explanation: A linear search algorithm checks each element in the list one by one, hence its time complexity is O(n).

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a recursive algorithm?
questions.explanation: Quicksort is a divide-and-conquer algorithm that uses recursion to sort the list of elements.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a switch statement in programming?
options: To control the flow of a program based on conditional statements
questions.explanation: A switch statement is used to control the flow of a program based on conditional statements, where the possible values are known beforehand.

questions.question: Which of the following data structures is best suited for storing a collection of unique elements?
questions.explanation: A Set is a data structure that stores a collection of unique elements and provides fast lookup, insertion, and deletion operations.

questions.question: What is the concept of a Big O notation?
options: A way to measure the size of a data structure
A way to measure the time complexity of an algorithm
questions.explanation: Big O notation is a way to measure the time complexity of an algorithm, which gives an upper bound on the number of steps the algorithm takes to complete.
